description: Crypto Trading System - Full autonomous cryptocurrency trading engine with market monitoring, technical analysis, signal generation, portfolio management, risk control, and exchange API integration. This is the money-making brain of MK.
steps: Create MK_OS/crypto/ directory for the entire crypto subsystem

Create MK_OS/crypto/market_data.cpp - Real-time price fetching from CoinGecko/Binance public APIs using libcurl. Class MKMarketData with methods: fetchPrice(symbol), fetchOHLC(symbol,interval), fetchOrderBook(symbol), fetchVolume(symbol). Store price history in memory for analysis. Include rate limiting.

Create MK_OS/crypto/technical_analysis.cpp - Class MKTechnicalAnalysis implementing: RSI (14-period), MACD (12,26,9), Bollinger Bands (20,2), EMA/SMA calculations, Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP), Support/Resistance detection, Moving Average crossover detection, Fibonacci retracement levels. All pure math on price arrays.

Create MK_OS/crypto/signal_engine.cpp - Class MKSignalEngine that combines technical indicators into buy/sell/hold signals. Implements: multi-timeframe analysis, signal strength scoring (0-100), signal history tracking, backtesting on historical data, false signal filtering. Each signal has confidence, reasoning text, and timestamp.

Create MK_OS/crypto/portfolio_manager.cpp - Class MKPortfolioManager tracking: holdings (coin, amount, avg_buy_price, current_value), total portfolio value, PnL per position, allocation percentages, rebalancing suggestions, DCA (Dollar Cost Averaging) schedule management. Persists to SQLite.

Create MK_OS/crypto/risk_manager.cpp - Class MKRiskManager enforcing: position size limits (never >20% in one coin), stop-loss levels, daily loss limits, drawdown protection (pause trading if portfolio drops >10% in 24h), volatility-adjusted position sizing, correlation-based diversification rules.

Create MK_OS/crypto/exchange_api.cpp - Class MKExchangeAPI with abstract interface for exchange operations: placeOrder(side,symbol,qty,type), cancelOrder(id), getBalance(), getOpenOrders(). Implementations for Binance (spot) using their REST API with HMAC-SHA256 signing. API keys loaded from config file.

Create MK_OS/crypto/trading_bot.cpp - Class MKTradingBot as the orchestrator: runs the autonomous trading loop (scan markets -> analyze -> generate signals -> check risk -> execute -> monitor). Includes paper trading mode (no real money), live trading mode, performance reporting, trade journal logging.

Create MK_OS/crypto/airdrop_farmer.cpp - Class MKAirdropFarmer that tracks upcoming airdrops, requirements to qualify, task completion status, estimated values. Maintains a list of protocols and their airdrop criteria.

Create MK_OS/crypto/crypto_knowledge.mk - Knowledge file with 500+ crypto facts: major coins, DeFi protocols, exchange info, trading strategies, blockchain concepts, historical events, market psychology terms.
Add #include directives for all crypto modules in system/mk_entry.cpp and add MKTradingBot to the MKSystem struct with proper initialization
Update the Makefile to list crypto source files in a comment for reference (build still works via single compilation unit)
acceptance_criteria: All crypto .cpp files compile without errors when included via mk_entry.cpp
make in MK_OS/ produces mk_os binary with zero errors
make test still passes all existing tests
MKTechnicalAnalysis correctly calculates RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands on sample data
MKRiskManager rejects orders that exceed position limits
MKTradingBot can run in paper trading mode without crashes
findings: All 12 steps completed successfully. Build produces mk_os binary with 7 warnings (all pre-existing unused-parameter warnings, none from crypto modules except one benign 'middle' param in signal_engine). All 11 existing tests pass (160 assertions). crypto_knowledge.mk has 593 pipe-delimited facts. Trading bot defaults to PAPER mode for safety. Exchange API requires BINANCE_API_KEY and BINANCE_API_SECRET env vars for live mode. The crypto_knowledge.mk is loaded via relative path from knowledge_dir (../../../crypto/crypto_knowledge.mk).
